title: Squadcast Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Squadcast is an incident management and on-call scheduling platform that provides alert routing, escalation policies, SLO tracking, status pages, and post-incident analytics. Squadcast is designed to reduce alert fatigue through intelligent deduplication and suppression, while ensuring critical incidents reach the right responders. The T1 Agentics integration enables SOC teams to create and manage security incidents, route alerts to on-call security personnel, and track incident resolution metrics.

**Vendor:** Squadcast
**Category:** Ticketing & Incident Management
**Authentication:** API Key (Refresh Token)

## Prerequisites

Before configuring the Squadcast integration in T1 Agentics, ensure you have:

- A Squadcast account (Free, Pro, Premium, or Enterprise plan)
- An API refresh token:
  - Navigate to **Settings > API Tokens** in the Squadcast dashboard
  - Generate a new API refresh token
- Required permissions:
  - **Account Owner** or **Admin** for full API access
  - **User** role with appropriate team assignment for incident management
- At least one service and escalation policy configured in Squadcast
- Your Squadcast API endpoint: `https://api.squadcast.com/v3`
- Network access from T1 Agentics to the Squadcast API

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics sidebar
2. Search for **Squadcast** or browse the **Ticketing** category
3. Click on the Squadcast card and select **Configure**
4. Enter your connection details:
   - **Connection Name**: A descriptive label (e.g., "Squadcast SOC")
   - **API Refresh Token**: Your Squadcast API refresh token
5. Click **Test Connection** to verify API access
6. Click **Save** to activate the integration
7. The integration will appear in your **Connect > Active Integrations** list

## Available Actions

| Action | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Create Incident | Generic | Create a new incident on a specific service |
| Get Incident | Investigate | Retrieve details of a specific incident |
| List Incidents | Investigate | List incidents with status and service filtering |
| Acknowledge Incident | Generic | Acknowledge a triggered incident |
| Resolve Incident | Generic | Resolve an active incident |
| Reassign Incident | Generic | Reassign an incident to a different user or team |
| Add Incident Note | Generic | Add a note to the incident timeline |
| List Services | Investigate | List all Squadcast services |
| List Escalation Policies | Investigate | List all escalation policies |
| List Schedules | Investigate | List all on-call schedules |
| Get On-Call | Investigate | Get the current on-call responder for a schedule |
| List Teams | Investigate | List all teams in the organization |

## Common Use Cases

- **Security alert ingestion**: Create Squadcast incidents from T1 Agentics for security alerts, routing them through escalation policies to reach on-call security analysts
- **Alert deduplication**: Leverage Squadcast's deduplication rules to prevent duplicate alerts for the same security event from overwhelming the SOC team
- **Incident reassignment**: Reassign security incidents between analysts based on expertise, availability, or the evolving nature of the incident
- **On-call management**: Query on-call schedules to understand team availability before routing security incidents or planning response activities
- **Resolution metrics tracking**: Track incident acknowledgment and resolution times to measure and improve SOC performance metrics

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| Token authentication fails | Squadcast uses refresh tokens to generate access tokens. Verify the refresh token is valid and has not been revoked |
| Incident creation fails | Ensure the service name or ID is correct and has an active escalation policy assigned |
| On-call returns no responder | Verify the on-call schedule has active rotations with participants assigned for the current time period |
| Duplicate incidents created | Configure deduplication rules in Squadcast service settings. Use consistent event identifiers to enable deduplication |
| Escalation not triggering | Verify the escalation policy has properly configured levels and timeouts. Ensure target users have notification rules configured |
| API rate limiting | Squadcast enforces rate limits. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff |
